questions.q: Which statement best distinguishes paging from segmentation?
options: Paging uses variable-size logical blocks; segmentation uses fixed-size blocks
Paging uses fixed-size blocks; segmentation uses variable-size logical blocks
Both use fixed-size blocks divided by the program structure
questions.explain: Paging divides memory into fixed-size pages/frames, whereas segmentation uses variable-size blocks based on the program's logical structure.

questions.q: Virtual memory allows a computer to run programs larger than its physical RAM by doing what?
options: Adding extra physical RAM chips automatically
Using secondary storage (a swap/page file) as if it were RAM
questions.explain: Virtual memory uses secondary storage as if it were RAM, swapping pages in and out, though it is slower than real RAM.

questions.q: When a higher-priority interrupt occurs, why can the CPU later resume the original task correctly?
options: The OS restarts the program from the beginning
The current registers (e.g. PC, ACC) are pushed onto a stack and popped back afterwards
The interrupt clears all registers permanently
The task is deleted and rescheduled
questions.explain: Register values are pushed onto a LIFO stack before the ISR runs and popped back afterwards, so the interrupted task resumes exactly.

questions.q: An air-traffic control system must always respond within a guaranteed deadline. Which OS type is required?
questions.explain: A real-time OS guarantees a response within a set time limit, which is essential for safety-critical control systems.

questions.q: Which scheduling algorithm gives each process a fixed time slice in turn and is pre-emptive?
questions.explain: Round robin gives each process a fixed quantum in turn and pre-empts those that do not finish, ensuring fairness and no starvation.

questions.q: Which is the first program to run when a computer is switched on?
options: The BIOS firmware, which runs POST and loads the OS
questions.explain: The BIOS firmware on ROM runs first, performs the Power-On Self-Test, then bootstraps the OS into RAM; it is not itself the OS.

questions.q: Why is Java bytecode described as portable, run by the JVM on any platform?
options: It is compiled directly to x86 machine code
It is platform-independent intermediate code executed by a virtual machine
It cannot be run without recompiling for each device
It is interpreted line by line from source each time
questions.explain: Source is compiled to platform-independent intermediate code (bytecode) that any device's process VM (e.g. the JVM) can run, giving write-once-run-anywhere portability.

questions.q: Which item is an example of utility software rather than application software?
questions.explain: Utility software maintains or optimises the computer itself; antivirus and defragmentation are utilities, not applications.

questions.q: Which statement about compilers and interpreters is correct?
options: A compiler translates and executes one line at a time at run time
An interpreter produces a standalone executable file
A compiler translates all the source at once and produces an executable
An interpreter is faster at run time because no translation is needed
questions.explain: A compiler translates the whole program at once into a standalone executable, whereas an interpreter translates and runs line by line with no executable.

questions.q: In which stage of compilation are syntax errors reported and a parse tree built?
questions.explain: Syntax analysis checks tokens against the language grammar, builds the parse tree and reports syntax errors.

questions.q: A dynamic linker differs from a static linker because it...
options: Copies all library code into the executable, making it larger
Links shared library code (e.g. a .dll) at run time, keeping the executable smaller
Removes the need for any libraries
questions.explain: Dynamic linking keeps libraries separate and links them at run time, so the executable is smaller and the shared library can be updated independently.

questions.q: A start-up has unclear requirements that will change as customers give feedback. Which methodology fits best?
options: A single linear pass with no iteration
None — coding should start with a fixed full spec
questions.explain: Agile uses iterative increments with continuous client feedback, ideal when requirements are unclear or likely to change.

questions.q: Using the addressing modes for operand 20 (where address 20 holds 35 and address 35 holds 99), what does direct addressing LDA 20 load into the ACC?
questions.explain: In direct addressing the operand is the address holding the value, so LDA 20 loads the contents of address 20, which is 35.

questions.q: Which OOP concept lets a subclass acquire the attributes and methods of a superclass to promote reuse?
questions.explain: Inheritance allows a subclass to acquire (and add to or override) a superclass's attributes and methods, supporting code reuse.
